questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $A=\left\{x \mid x^3+x^2-p x+q=0, p, q, \in R\right\}$ and $B=\left\{x \mid x^2-q x+2=0, q \in R\right\}$ be the sets. If $n(A \cap B)=2$ and $x_0 \in(A-B)$, then find the value of $\left|p-q+x_0\right|$.
[Note: $n(P \cap Q)$ denotes number of common elements in set $P$ and set $Q$ and $a \in(P-Q)$ denotes elements ' $a$ ' lies in set $P$ not in set $Q$.]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & & \left(x-x_0\right)\left(x^2-q x+2\right) & =x^3+x^2-p x+q \\ \Rightarrow & & -x_0-q & =1,2+q x_0=-p,-2 x_0=q \\ \Rightarrow & & x_0 & =1, q=-2, p=0 \\ \therefore & & \left|p-q+x_0\right| & =3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A survey shows that <math><mn>69</mn><mi>%</mi></math> students like mathematics, whereas <math><mn>75</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> like chemistry. If <math><mi>x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></math> students like both the subjects, then the maximum value of <math><mi>x</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Let, total students in survey be 100

If mathematics is represented by m & chemistry by c, then 
nm∩c=x
Given, nm=69, nc=75
Now, nm∪c=nm+nc-nm∩c
=69+75–x
=144–x
∵75≤nm∪c≤100
75≤144-x ≤100
∴ 44≤x≤69
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>50</mn><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, where <math><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math> is the set of all integers, is
questions.solution:
a2+b2=50
a=1⇒b2=49
⇒b=±7
a=2,3,4,6 do not give integer value of b .
a=5⇒b2=25⇒b=±5
a=7⇒b2=1
⇒b=±1
Similarly,
a=-1⇒b=±7
a=-5⇒b=±5
a=-7→b=±1
⇒ The required number of elements (a,b)=12
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P=\left\{x \mid x^2+(n-1) x-2(n+1)=0\right\}$ and $Q=\left\{(n-1) x^2+n x+1=0\right\}$. Then find the number of values of $n$ such that $P \cup Q$ has exactly 3 distinct elements (where $x$ is a real number).
questions.solution:
Notice that $x=2$ is a root of $P$ because $2^2+(n-1) \cdot 2-2(n+1)=0$ and $x=-1$ is a root of $Q$ because $(n-1) \cdot(-1)^2+n \cdot(-1)+1=0$.
Therefore, we see that $P(x)=(x-2)(x+n+1)$ and $Q(x)=(x+1)((n-1) x+1)$
If $|P \cup Q|=3$, then $P(x)$ and $Q(x)$ share a root.
Therefore, we have the three equations:
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 & =-\left(\frac{1}{n-1}\right) \Rightarrow n=\frac{1}{2}-1=-n-1 \Rightarrow n=0 \\
-\left(\frac{1}{n-1}\right) & =-n-1 \Rightarrow n= \pm \sqrt{2}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, there are total of 7 possible values of $n$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
